Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Room joining failures - persistence restore, connection error handling, Fly.io 1GB RAM

Room joins were failing with "not found" because rooms are in-memory and lost on
server restart. Persistence saved snapshots to Redis but never restored them.
After the error, users were stuck on "Waiting for room state..." forever because
the error auto-cleared after 5 seconds with no recovery path.

- Restore rooms from Redis persistence on JOIN_ROOM when not found in memory
- Add room code -> roomId index in Redis for lookup by code
- Persist ROOM_NOT_FOUND and CONNECTION_FAILED errors (no auto-clear)
- Fix Socket.IO reconnection status (show spinner, not "waiting")
- Show actionable error screens with "Back to Home" / "Retry" buttons
- Scale Fly.io RAM from 512MB to 1024MB to reduce OOM restarts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add persistent HTTPS keep-alive connections for S3

Eliminates per-request TLS handshake overhead by reusing connections.
New s3/connection.ss implements HTTP/1.1 keep-alive with chunked
transfer encoding. S3Client gains keep-alive?: keyword (default #t)
with automatic fallback to single-use connections on failure.

Also handles Content-Encoding gzip decompression for persistent
connections (matching :std/net/request behavior) and uses resp-*
adapter functions so callers work transparently with both paths.

commit.message:
feat: CSV post-import enrichment + per-row distress detection + $490K ARV cap

- Add 7 distress boolean fields to SentinelField (deceased_owner, bankruptcy,
  divorce, foreclosure, site_vacant, owner_occupied, tax_delinquent) with
  auto-mapping patterns for PropertyRadar CSV columns
- CSV import now reads per-row boolean columns and creates targeted distress
  events with proper severity (probate=9, bankruptcy=8, etc.)
- Hard ARV filter at $490K: property records are kept but no lead is created
  for properties above the cap (too expensive for wholesale buyer pool)
- New /api/ingest/csv-post-enrich endpoint: batch PR API enrichment that takes
  propertyIds from csv-upload response and runs the full pipeline (PR lookup →
  signal detection → scoring → auto-promote to prospect)
- csv-upload response now includes importedPropertyIds array for chaining
- PropertyRadar from-list endpoint: add fallback strategy for list item fetching

commit.message:
Cache user in localStorage so welcome greeting shows name reliably

The welcome greeting was showing "Good Afternoon" without the user's
name even when signed in, because the session API call hadn't resolved
by the time the greeting rendered. Now the user object is cached in
localStorage on successful session check, and getUser() falls back to
the cached copy when the in-memory value isn't set yet.

Implement core modules: store, relay hub, agent daemon, hook shim

internal/store/sqlite.go  — full SQLite Store implementation with schema
  migrations, all CRUD operations for users, auth tokens, device auth,
  hosts, sessions, session events, approval requests, settings

internal/relay/hub.go     — WebSocket hub routing PTY output and approval
  messages between host agents and browser clients; graceful shutdown

internal/agent/daemon.go  — host daemon connecting outbound to relay with
  exponential backoff reconnect; Unix socket listener for hook shim calls;
  approval request/response lifecycle

internal/agent/session.go — PTY session pool: spawn, stream output as
  binary frames, write stdin, kill

cmd/agentcockpit/hook.go  — hook shim wired to daemon Unix socket;
  blocks until user decision; risk classification heuristic; fail-open
  if daemon unavailable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace panicking expect/unwrap in FFI and GPU paths (#772) (#782)

* fix: replace panicking expect/unwrap in FFI and GPU paths with proper error handling (#772)

GPU evaluation: replace 6 `.expect()` calls on device availability with
`.context()?` so GPU unavailability returns `Err` instead of panicking.

FFI serialisation: create shared `ffi::helpers` module with `to_ffi_json`,
`ffi_error_literal`, and `panic_to_ffi_json` helpers that handle errors
gracefully. Replace ~40 `unwrap()` call sites across all FFI entry points,
eliminating panic vectors at the FFI boundary.

All FFI functions retain `panic::catch_unwind` as a safety net.

commit.message:
feat: TARS Robot State API — state bridge pre OpenClaw control

Pridané REST endpointy v main.py:
  GET  /robot/state   → aktuálny stav (explorer, last_scene)
  POST /robot/explore → zapni/vypni explorer vzdialene
  POST /robot/stop    → núdzové zastavenie + RTM stop cmd
  POST /robot/scene   → dashboard tu posiela YOLO detekcie
  GET  /robot/scene   → TARS sa pýta čo robot vidí

Dashboard syncronizuje stav:
  - explorerToggle() POSTuje do /robot/explore
  - visionLoop() POSTuje detekcie do /robot/scene po každom frame
  - pollRobotState() polling /robot/state každé 2s, synkronizuje explorer

TARS (OpenClaw cez Telegram/WhatsApp) môže teraz:
  - Zapnúť exploráciu: POST /robot/explore {"enabled": true}
  - Zastaviť robota:   POST /robot/stop
  - Vidieť čo robot vidí: GET /robot/scene

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude: abort sign-out UI if Firebase recovered during presignout delay

Fixes welcome-back banner showing while signed in, and Chrome's native
'Reload site?' dialog appearing unexpectedly.

Root cause: _doSignOutUIInner() ran after _runPresignout() delay even
when Firebase had recovered the session during that window, briefly
showing the sign-in panel (with its checkbox input) and triggering
Chrome's form-protection beforeunload dialog.

Fix: bail at top of _doSignOutUIInner if auth.currentUser is set.
